title: podman remote send tar
body: It'is a pr about the issue #8374

comments.body:
Could you rebase your commit, looks like you submitted a merge PR with your code.

Also you need to sign your PR.

git rebase -i origin
git commit --amend -s -a
git push --force

```
Skipped directories tunnel: pkg/api
pkg/bindings/images/build.go:264:2: Consider preallocating `excludes` (prealloc)
	var excludes []string
	^
pkg/bindings/images/build.go:220:14: ineffectual assignment to `err` (ineffassign)
			excludes, err := parseDockerignore(s)
			          ^
pkg/bindings/images/build.go:322:13: S1003: should use strings.Contains(".$", string(ch)) instead (gosimple)
		} else if strings.Index(".$", string(ch)) != -1 {
```
The format is a bit problematic

interesting fails
1) the test code at  https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/eaf7a26b3c8a129ff429841453f2827d6bc34d85/test/e2e/build_test.go#L197 has a bug. when inspect fails it should not continue, however it access the data[0] and panics.

2) build succeeded, however inspect failed.
```
         Running: podman-remote [options] build build/basicalpine --iidfile /tmp/podman_test185355255/idFile
         STEP 1: FROM alpine
         Completed short name "alpine" with unqualified-search registries (origin: /etc/containers/registries.conf)
[+0800s] Getting image source signatures
         Copying blob sha256:9d16cba9fb961d1aafec9542f2bf7cb64acfc55245f9e4eb5abecd4cdc38d749
[+0801s] Copying config sha256:961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4
         Writing manifest to image destination
         Storing signatures
         STEP 2: COMMIT
         --> 96176967641
         961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4
         Running: podman-remote [options] inspect 961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4
         
         []
         Error: error inspecting object: no such object: "961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4\n"
```

I don't think they are related to me, am I wrong?
seem iidfile not work with this

> seem iidfile not work with this

No, It is not about iidfile. iidfile succeeded. podman-remote execute inspect command with contents of iidfile.  I also use this patched version at my envrionment without any exception. 

And, an interesting thing is, I did not understand why this test https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/eaf7a26b3c8a129ff429841453f2827d6bc34d85/test/e2e/build_test.go#L175 have been run 3 times. It'is the failed test. Firstly, I think it is about default behavior for failed tests, however the other failed tests have been run only once. 

It'is possible to get podman system service log file at debug level for that test? In my environment I cannot reproduce that.
@rhatdan  PTAL

It looks like the tests never checks if inspect succeeds.

You need to add this, to make sure it succeeds, which I believe it did not.
		Expect(session.ExitCode()).To(Equal(0))

"961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4\n"
I believe the problem is the extra "\n" in the iidfile.
sorry for that,the git append all commits. :(

after rebase from upstream (this repo), the remote tests still fails with 

```
Error: error inspecting object: no such object: "961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4\n"
```

the iidfile contains extra \n at end and of it. and inspect fails. What should I do now?
/assign @zhangguanzhang

I found the problem and fixed. it.

static build failed, but I do not think it is really about this pr.

> after rebase from upstream (this repo), the remote tests still fails with
> 
> ```
> Error: error inspecting object: no such object: "961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4\n"
> ```
> 
> the iidfile contains extra \n at end and of it. and inspect fails. What should I do now?

also i figure out the problem, rebase had created a conflict with commit https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/b5e15f18d01219f666cd30623fb3ec73f338d040 hence the tests were failed.
need add a test unit for it

> need add a test unit for it

ok, I can write tests. however I didn't understand tests folders structure to put unit tests for changes. Can you give some advice?

> > need add a test unit for it
> 
> ok, I can write tests. however I didn't understand tests folders structure to put unit tests for changes. Can you give some advice?

Is it enough to create a test case inside https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/test/e2e/build_test.go for building some dockers with dummy content?

I did not find a kind of tests for per function like build_test.go for functions inside build.go

however for package images there is a file https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/pkg/bindings/test/images_test.go however this test file did not contain functions for Build function https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/pkg/bindings/images/build.go#L29
Yes the e2e tests is ok.

> Yes the e2e tests is ok.

@rhatdan 

I planed these tests:

1) build container/dockerfile inside not root of contextdir.

2a) put .containerignore file for filtering (both file and folder ignoring)
2b) put .dockerignore file for filtering (both file and folder ignoring)

priority of .containerignore will be more than .dockerignore.

Is there a easy way to check layer contents? for ignore I need to check layer content.

```
FROM alpine:latest
ADD . /testignore/ #need to check this layer's content
```
I think these tests are enough. However I am open to advises.
SGTM

@rhatdan while I am working on tests I found this line is interesting: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/test/e2e/build_test.go#L237

the -f parameter has arg Containerfile.path but it is inside **build/basicalpine** , I think parameter should be **build/basicalpine/Containerfile.path** like at https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/test/e2e/build_test.go#L203

I will also fix that.

However if its a feature I creates a ambiguity like:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1861031/103159736-ffce8080-47dd-11eb-9ba2-e6fd565f93ec.png)

which containerfile will be used?

OK, now podman-remote build behavior is nearly same as docker. Only this kind of builds are failing:
Let assume folder hierarchy is same as below

```
.
├── Containerfile
└── test1
    ├── Containerfile -> ../Containerfile
    ├── hello
    ├── hello2
    ├── hello3 -> hello2
    └── subdir
```

These docker builds commands works well
```
docker build -t test -f test1/Containerfile test1
cd test1
docker build -t test -f Containerfile .
```

However podman failes beacause a depedency forcing symlink targets should be inside context dir. The related source line is: 

https://github.com/containers/storage/blob/44cf7aab45e8f84f0aca3766f6e485288d349af4/pkg/archive/archive.go#L668-L672

So I did not implement this kind of builds.

Also:
A test  named as **int podman fedora-32 root host** failed. however it's blood brothers **int podman fedora-33 root host**, **int podman ubuntu-2004 root host** and **int podman ubuntu-2010 root host** passed. I didn't understand why.

@zhangguanzhang can you trigger failed and cancelled tests again?
I'm on a business trip these days and may need someone else to do these things
you could clikc the detail and `View more details on Cirrus CI` Cirrus CI website, and you could see the button which named `re-run`
@vrothberg @mheon @baude @jwhonce PTAL

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** Approval requirements bypassed by manually added approval. This pull-request has been approved by: *kazimsarikaya* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@kazimsarikaya Podman should support -f.  Pretty much we should support everything that docker build does, if not it is a bug or should be documented as a difference.

> @kazimsarikaya Podman should support -f. Pretty much we should support everything that docker build does, if not it is a bug or should be documented as a difference.

ok, podman remote does not support -f parameter as following symlinks and send send them separately from context dir.
@kazimsarikaya Could you open an issue on that, or maybe better a PR?  :^)

@rhatdan i can fix that issue with with pr too. because i also handle several docker -f differences with this pr.

also i am waiting an answer too. I commented on reviews however i didnot get proper answer. The problem is that:

* buildah has a internal parameter, not exists in buildconfig, --ignorefile 
* buildah parses that parameter before creating buildconfig and appends that ignore lines to buildconfig's excluedes parameter. 
* however podman did not achive that. 

I mention that at comment: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8456#discussion_r551134761 also @TomSweeneyRedHat  asked that at https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8456#discussion_r551542859 and I also  replied with the same question.

I can easily to perform changes to  the cmd  before https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/cmd/podman/images/build.go#L403
LGTM
/lgtm
/hold
@rhatdan i am work on --ignorefile argument and making docker -f compilant. probably today i will finished them.
@kazimsarikaya We can do that as a follow up PR.  As long as there are no changes to CLI and API. Today is feature freeze.

ok then i will make them a new pr. 
/hold cancel
